name: argo
description: Argo's portable engineering 'way of working' for Claude Code — opinionated but non-breaking. Ships active: on-demand discipline skills, full-lifecycle agent roles (including a product role + write-prd skill that author a lightweight grounded PRD — the durable WHAT/WHY — at the top of the loop, with checkable requirements, a feature→screen matrix that downstream design/code completeness gates consume, and a user-agreed ASCII wireframe + flow per screen — the sole layout-intent artifact feeding the designer; the Figma lo-fi wireframing stage is removed entirely), a Figma-to-code design pack shaped as a mechanism (provider-neutral core) plus swappable recipes — one recipe implemented today, shadcn-tailwind — (setup-design/figma-audit/figma-sync/design-component/figma-to-code/design-upgrade; the deterministic design-kit modules, skill-script CLI verbs, and reporters ship in the @argohq/toolkit package this plugin's hooks dispatch through), with a single-file Figma starter as the design source (duplicated once per project, all variables local, theme = modes on the file's own Semantic collection) plus a generated semantic token manifest LLM sessions author against, a twelfth design-rules mechanism rule (D24 gap/padding spacing) and a thirteenth (untraced-copy: every TEXT node traces to the wave copy deck or a registry defaultStrings entry), plus the design-phase decision gate (pre-build binding manifest + 'argo design validate-manifest' against registry existence, the committed confusable-pairs table, and the Always/Ask-first/Never guardrail), and five hook categories — plugin-side safety guardrails (dangerous-git, pipe-to-shell, lockfile-edit, bash-source-write, always on), and toolkit-dispatched gates via hooks/toolkit-dispatch.mjs → 'argo-hook' (fail-closed when a project declares @argohq/toolkit but it is missing; allow-with-warning before a project is argo-initialized, so bootstrap can proceed): self-scoping build gates (red-proof, trust — armed only during gated builds via .argo/evidence/build-mode.json, inert otherwise), design-commit-gate (armed per-app by .argo/config.json design blocks, independent of build-mode, enforcing fresh passing spec-diff receipts for componentsPath commits), a generic playbook-permission gate (playbook-engine phase 1 — runs on every tool call, enforcing an active playbook's per-stage allow-list and a protected-path deny list; supersedes the retired design-guard record/stop hooks, with design-rules audit enforcement now owned by pack-design's design-rules-check gate), write hygiene (format-on-write, test-smell), plus a session-start way-of-working card. Opinionated rules ship inert as templates the init skill installs adapted to the host project's stack. Works in any project, with or without the Argo cockpit. See README.
